steps.description: Click on the "Teams Agents and Apps" option within the dropdown menu under the "New Project" dialog in the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit interface.
Click on the "Other Teams Capability" option within the dropdown menu titled "Teams Agent or App Using Teams AI Library" in the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit interface.
Click the "Simple Bot" option in the "Teams Capability" dropdown menu.
Click the "JavaScript" option in the "Programming Language" dropdown within the Microsoft Visual Studio Code interface.
Click the "Default folder" option (/home/vscode/AgentsToolkitProjects) in the "Workspace Folder" dropdown within the Visual Studio Code interface.
Press the F1 key to activate the command palette in the Microsoft Visual Studio Code interface, specifically in the "Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit" context.

Type text into the command palette input field: 'Notifications: Clear All Notifications' to select the corresponding command option from the dropdown list in the Microsoft 365 plugin interface within Visual Studio Code.
Press "Enter" to select the "Notifications: Clear All Notifications" option in the Visual Studio Code Command Palette while using the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit extension interface.
Press the F1 key to open the Command Palette in the Visual Studio Code interface.
Type text into the Visual Studio Code command palette field: 'Debug: Select and Start Debugging'.
Press the "Enter" key to confirm the "Debug: Select and Start Debugging" option in the Visual Studio Code command palette.
Click the "Debug" option in the dropdown menu within the top command palette of Visual Studio Code to select a debug configuration.
Type text: 'Debug in Teams (Chrome)' into the command palette search bar in Microsoft Visual Studio Code, filtering the list of options displayed under the "Debug" category.
Press the "Enter" key to select the highlighted option "Debug in Teams (Chrome)" from the dropdown in the debug configuration panel of Visual Studio Code.
Click on the "Email, phone, or Skype" input field within the Microsoft sign-in page to focus the cursor and enable text entry.
Click "+" to open new page
Type URL 'https://admin.teams.microsoft.com/policies/manage-apps' into the browser's address bar located at the top of the Google Chrome interface on the 'New Tab' page.
Press the "Enter" key to select the URL "https://admin.teams.microsoft.com/policies/manage-apps" from the browser address bar dropdown in Google Chrome.
Type text: ${{env:M365_ACCOUNT_ADMIN_ACCOUNT}} into the 'Email or phone' input field on the Microsoft Sign-In page.
Press the "Enter" key to confirm the email address entered in the "Sign in" field on the Microsoft login page and proceed by initiating the "Next" button action.
Type the text ${{secret:M365_ACCOUNT_ADMIN_PASSWORD}} into the password input field on the Microsoft login page displayed at `login.microsoftonline.com`.
Press the "Enter" key to confirm the password input and interact with the "Sign in" button on the Microsoft login page.
Click the "Yes" button in the "Stay signed in?" prompt on the Microsoft login page (login.microsoftonline.com) to decline staying signed in.
@assertion all three numeric values under “Apps to consider allowing” are displayed
Close the previous page
Click the "Search" input field in the Microsoft Teams admin center at coordinates (426, 106).
Type text: ${{var:app_name}}  into the search bar located at the top of the Microsoft Teams Admin Center interface, under the header "Manage apps."
Type text: 'dev' into the search bar at the top right of the "Manage apps" page in the Microsoft Teams admin center.
Click on the "vscuse_app_QXMvdev" search result in the dropdown list located beneath the search bar in the Microsoft Teams Admin Center interface.
@assertion ${{var:app_name}}dev exist
Click the "Publish" button in the Microsoft Teams admin center under the app details for "vscuse_app_K4tt4dev" to initiate app publishing.
Click the "Publish" button in the "Publish your custom app?" confirmation dialog within the Microsoft Teams admin center application interface.
@assertion all three numeric values under “Apps to consider allowing” are displayed
Click the "Microsoft Teams admin center" logo in the top-left corner of the header to navigate to the dashboard home.
Type text: 'https://teams.microsoft.com/v2/' into the address bar of the Google Chrome browser in a New Tab page interface.
Press the "Enter" key to navigate to the URL "https://teams.microsoft.com/v2/" displayed in the address bar of the Chrome browser.
Click the "Apps" button in the left sidebar of the Microsoft Teams web interface, identified by a plus sign and labeled "Apps" on hover.
@assertion 'Built for your org' is visible

Type text: ${{var:app_name}}  into the "Search apps and more" input field located in the left sidebar under the "Apps" section of the Microsoft Teams application, as displayed in the cloud-based Teams web interface.
Type text: 'dev' into the search bar located within the "Apps" section of the Microsoft Teams interface to filter available apps.
Press the "Enter" key to confirm the search query in the "Search (Ctrl+Alt+E)" input field located in the "Apps" section of Microsoft Teams.
Click on the app card labeled "vscuse_app_hmUwdev" in the "Apps" search results within the Microsoft Teams "Manage apps" interface.
@assertion  ${{var:app_name}}dev is visible

Click the "Add" button next to the app "vscuse_app_hmUwcdev" in the Microsoft Teams "Manage apps" interface. This button is located on the right side of the app listing, within the modal dialog displaying app details.
Click the "Open" button in the "Added successfully!" confirmation dialog for the app `vscuse_app_qKaXUdev` in the Microsoft Teams web interface.
Click the "Hi" button in the application interface to initiate a greeting to the bot.
Click inside the input text field at the bottom of the Microsoft Teams application interface to type a message.
Press the "Enter" key to send the message "Hi" in the chat input field of the Microsoft Teams app interface.
@assertion the bot responded and not return error code
